title: AN APPROACH TO ORGANIZING GROUP DISCUSSIONS IN EFL LEARNING / UNE APPROCHE POUR ORGANISER DES DISCUSSIONS DE GROUPE DANS L'APPRENTISSAGE DE L'ANGLAIS LANGUE ETRANGERE
author: Nicoleta Florina MINCǍ
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The paper aims to present group discussions as an efficient means for raising the students’ motivation level and increasing their involvement in the language learning process. Problem-solving discussion is a task-based activity viewed as a structural language endeavor, which leads to a range of outcomes for those undertaking the task. An organizational framework plays the key role for determining successful discussions. There are procedural steps of the group discussion sessions: the chairperson is chosen; the teacher selects the discussion topics; after formulating solutions, the chairperson directs the students to give an oral summary of the discussion proceedings; the teacher acts as a language consultant. Taking into consideration the students’ individual needs, we may use some techniques in order to deal with the uncooperative students.

keywords: effective discussions, problem-solving, communicative activities, procedural steps, techniques, teacher's participation
